message:
feat(tools): add archive-aware read pools for MCP tool read operations and health check semantic readiness

Introduce archive-aware read pools (ToolReadPool, ResourceReadPool) for MCP tool
operations that only need read access to the mailbox. These pools detect when the
archive is ahead of the live SQLite index and transparently serve reads from an
archive-reconstructed snapshot, preventing stale data from reaching agents.

Key changes:

- get_read_db_pool() in tool_util returns a ToolReadPool that wraps either the
  live pool or an archive-reconstructed snapshot pool based on drift detection
- list_contacts, search_messages_product, fetch_inbox_product,
  summarize_thread_product, and resource reads switched from get_db_pool() to
  get_read_db_pool() for read-only operations
- identity.rs gains comprehensive health_check_semantic_readiness probe that
  validates SQLite file health, schema presence, connectivity, and archive
  parity without initializing the mailbox -- this is a non-destructive health
  assessment suitable for MCP health_check tool calls
- resources.rs gains its own ResourceReadPool with the same drift detection and
  snapshot fallback pattern, independently scoped from the tools-layer pool
- search.rs updated for structural changes

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
